items.scenario: You are a member of the Maplewood Community Theater and signed up for its eight-week stage-design workshop. After two sessions, you have noticed that the instructor frequently arrives twenty minutes late, which has cut the hands-on building portion significantly. You want to reach the program coordinator before the next class.
items.direction: Write an email to the program coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the late-start pattern in the first two sessions
Explain how the lost time has affected the building exercises
Suggest a way to make up the missed instruction
items.subject: Late starts in the stage-design workshop

items.scenario: You are a junior writing your senior thesis on regional water policy. Your advisor recommended interviewing a state planning official, but you have struggled to secure a response. You want to ask your advisor for help refining your approach and possibly making an introduction before the spring deadline.
items.direction: Write an email to your thesis advisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the outreach attempts you have already made
Explain why a state-level interview matters for your argument
Ask whether your advisor can introduce you to a contact
items.subject: Help reaching a state planning interviewee

items.scenario: You ordered a refurbished tablet from an online electronics retailer two weeks ago for a remote field course. When it arrived, the stylus pen listed in the bundle was missing and the battery now drains within three hours of moderate use. You want to contact the retailer's customer-support manager.
items.direction: Write an email to the customer-support manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the missing stylus and the battery problem
Explain why the device is unusable for your field course
Request a replacement or full refund within a week
items.subject: Missing stylus and battery issue on order TB-90217

items.scenario: You just finished a part-time research assistantship at a campus archaeology lab, where you helped catalog excavation finds for the summer. Your supervisor mentioned a paid winter project on radiocarbon dating, and you want to express interest before the recruitment window closes next month.
items.direction: Write an email to your former lab supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Mention what you learned during the cataloging project this summer
Explain why the radiocarbon dating work fits your goals
Ask about the next steps for joining the winter project
items.subject: Interest in the winter radiocarbon dating project
